brands.name: Lennox
brands.notes: Premium brand — Signature / Elite / Merit product lines

brands.name: Armstrong Air
brands.notes: Allied Air sub-brand. Likely shares Platform 1/2 hardware with Lennox but public per-brand code dictionaries are thinner.

brands.name: AirEase
brands.notes: Allied Air sub-brand. Likely shares Platform 1/2 hardware with Lennox.

brands.name: Ducane
brands.notes: Allied Air value sub-brand (Lennox-owned Ducane, distinct from the older pre-2005 Ducane sold to Goodman).

structural_overview: Lennox International residential furnace diagnostics span four distinct generations: (1) SureLight 2-LED LEGACY — dual-LED integrated furnace control on mid-2000s through early-2010s furnaces (G50DF, G61MP/G61MPV, ML180UH). Uses COMPOUND X+Y flash codes (e.g., '1+2', '2+3', '4+7'), NOT simple single-digit flash counts. Also uses Pulse and Heartbeat named status patterns. (2) 7-SEGMENT HIGH-EFFICIENCY IFC — single-digit alphanumeric display on EL296V, SL280V, SL297V, SLP99V and variants. Namespace redesign: status characters ('.', 'A', 'C', 'H', 'h', 'dF', 'U') plus E-codes spanning E105 through E370+ with soft-lockout derivatives E270-E276. Communicating-capable via RSBus. (3) LOW-GWP / RDS-ERA — K-revision furnaces (EL195UHNEK, ML196UHEK, SLP99UHVK) retain the 7-segment display and inherit most legacy E-codes, BUT add genuinely new A2L refrigerant detection family: E150, E151, E152, E154, E155, E160, E161, E163, E164, E390. (4) iCOMFORT / S30/S40 COMMUNICATING ECOSYSTEM — three-surface architecture: wall thermostat shows numeric alert codes 10-39 for commissioning/config and 400+ for equipment faults; outdoor inverter units generate inverter flash codes that bridge to thermostat alert numbers — a single event may carry THREE linked IDs (thermostat number + inverter flash count + red/green LED flash pattern); communicating indoor equipment inherits Platform 2 E-codes. Public documentation is thinner than Lennox SureLight or 7-segment — the public Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide is curated rather than exhaustive. Allied Air cross-brand aliasing for Platforms 1 and 2 is LIKELY on shared hardware but public per-brand documentation is substantially thinner than Lennox's — treat carryover as platform-likely, not per-code-verified.

field_quirks.quirk: SureLight uses COMPOUND X+Y flash codes, not single-digit counts — a red LED flashing '1 pause 2' is code '1+2', not code '12'
field_quirks.applies_to: G61MPV and similar SureLight 2-LED legacy boards
field_quirks.reason: The earliest analysis of SureLight diagnostic tables frequently simplified to single-digit counts (e.g., '4 flashes = limit open'). The actual public G61MPV Installation Instructions code table publishes codes as compound patterns: '1+2' (low flame current run), '2+3' (low pressure switch failed open), '4+1' (watchguard exceeded retries), '5+2' (hard lockout board fault). Technicians counting single flashes miss the structure entirely. Also includes named status states 'Pulse' (standby) and 'Heartbeat' (heat demand active) that are not numeric codes at all.

field_quirks.quirk: SureLight watchguard timing is publicly specified on ML180UH
field_quirks.applies_to: ML180UH and SureLight boards with similar heating sequence
field_quirks.reason: Lennox ML180UH public heating sequence documents: 2.5 minutes to prove pressure switch closure, 5-minute reset period for pressure-switch watchguard, and five consecutive failed flame-proving tries lead to 60-MINUTE watchguard reset. Customers frequently call after exactly 60 minutes thinking furnace fixed itself — it's watchguard auto-reset, not a fix.

field_quirks.quirk: 7-segment IFC has a pushbutton workflow with 'E', 'c', 'F' modes
field_quirks.reason: Pushbutton menu: 'E' displays the most recent 10 error codes from memory; 'c' clears error memory; 'F' enters flame-signal mode showing live microamp reading on the 7-segment display. Critical for diagnosing weak-flame issues — no multimeter needed at the sensor if the tech knows to invoke F-mode.

field_quirks.quirk: E311 reduces firing rate in 60-second steps to match available CFM rather than locking out
field_quirks.applies_to: 7-segment high-efficiency IFCs on two-stage and modulating furnaces
field_quirks.reason: Unlike Carrier/Trane which typically hard-lock on airflow restriction, Lennox's 7-segment E311 throttles down. Two-stage controls drop to first stage; modulating controls reduce firing rate every 60 seconds until airflow matches. This is an ADAPTIVE response, not a fault lockout.

field_quirks.quirk: Low-GWP code E154/E155 latches blower for minimum 5 minutes even after sensor recovers
field_quirks.applies_to: EL195UHNEK, ML196UHEK, and other K-revision A2L refrigerant-compliant furnaces
field_quirks.reason: When refrigerant leak detector sensor loses communication (E154/E155), Lennox's A2L safety sequence latches the blower ON for at least 5 minutes regardless of thermostat call. This is a code-MANDATED behavior, not a thermostat call.

field_quirks.quirk: iComfort alert 429+ and inverter flash patterns are MULTIPLE IDs for the SAME EVENT on different surfaces
field_quirks.applies_to: iComfort S30/S40 with communicating inverter outdoor units (XC20, XC25, XP20, XP25)
field_quirks.reason: A single inverter event generates a thermostat alert number (e.g., 434) AND an outdoor-unit inverter LED flash count (e.g., Flash 53) AND a specific red/green LED flash pattern (Red 5 / Green 3). Lennox public alert guide 100017c formats them as '434 / Inverter Flash 53 / Red 5 Green 3'. Techs checking only one surface miss reset-procedure specificity.

field_quirks.quirk: iComfort alert 38/39 trigger a 60-minute offline retry, not an immediate lockout
field_quirks.applies_to: iComfort S30/S40 with any communicating equipment
field_quirks.reason: If the system cannot start heating (alert 38) or cooling (alert 39) for more than 45 minutes, thermostat marks 'System Going Offline' for 60 minutes and attempts automatic restart. Distinguishes from true lockouts that require manual reset.

field_quirks.quirk: A2L refrigerant detection cannot be disabled on K-revision low-GWP furnaces
field_quirks.applies_to: EL195UHNEK, ML196UHEK, SLP99UHVK and other K-revision A2L-compliant furnaces
field_quirks.reason: Lennox public K-revision documentation explicitly states that refrigerant detection functionality cannot be disabled on A2L systems — Lennox states disabling it is prohibited by safety codes. A tech attempting to bypass the RDS sensor circuit to silence repeated E154/E155 codes is creating a safety code violation. If RDS sensors fail repeatedly, the fix is sensor replacement, not bypass.

field_quirks.quirk: iComfort outdoor inverter faults publish THREE linked identifiers — store all three together
field_quirks.applies_to: Lennox communicating inverter outdoor units with iComfort S30/S40/E30
field_quirks.reason: Public Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c formats these alerts with three IDs linked per event: thermostat alert number, inverter flash count, and exact red/green LED flash pattern. Reset procedures can also differ between service-soon and service-urgent states within the same alert code — always preserve all three identifiers.

platforms.platform_description: Lennox SureLight integrated furnace control with dual LEDs (red diagnostic + green status/CFM). Uses COMPOUND X+Y flash codes where X flashes, then a pause, then Y flashes — e.g., '2+3' means 2 flashes pause 3 flashes. Named status patterns (Pulse = standby, Heartbeat = heat demand active) are not numeric. Covers G50DF, G61MP/G61MPV, ML180UH and similar mid-2000s through early-2010s Lennox furnaces. Allied Air equivalents on shared SureLight boards likely alias these codes but public per-brand documentation is thin.
platforms.visual_identification: Two LEDs on integrated furnace control board: one red diagnostic LED and one green status/CFM LED. Red LED flashes compound X+Y fault patterns (e.g., '1 flash, pause, 2 flashes' = code '1+2'). Green LED shows status patterns — Pulse (1 Hz blink) = standby, Heartbeat (steady rhythmic) = heat demand active.
platforms.platform_notes: Public sources: Lennox G61MPV Installation Instructions p.63 diagnostic table, Lennox ML180UH heating sequence pp.40-41. Compound-code format critical for accurate identification — a tech counting 'total red flashes' misreads the code entirely. Allied Air brands likely share SureLight platform on equivalent-era furnaces but public per-brand code tables are not as explicitly documented as Lennox.

diagnostic_path: Confirm thermostat is not calling for heat
If unit should be heating, verify W input and call path
codes.reset_behavior: Not a fault; state changes when operating demand changes.
codes.source: Lennox G61MPV Installation Instructions, diagnostic table, p.63

codes.meaning: Normal operation; heating demand initiated at thermostat
diagnostic_path: Verify sequence proceeds past inducer / pressure proving / ignition
codes.reset_behavior: Not a fault.
codes.source: Lennox G61MPV Installation Instructions, diagnostic table, p.63

diagnostic_path: Per Lennox ML180UH heating sequence, proof target is 0.5 µA within 4 seconds after gas valve opens, and 1.5 µA or greater in run sequence
Clean or replace sensor if weak
Inspect burner carryover and gas delivery
codes.escalation: Advisory; does not by itself stop operation per published sequence
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when flame signal returns to acceptable range.
codes.source: Lennox G61MPV diagnostic table, p.63; Lennox ML180UH heating sequence, pp.40-41

codes.meaning: Flame sensed out of sequence — flame still present
diagnostic_path: Verify flame is not present with valve de-energized
codes.escalation: Faulted state until abnormal flame condition is gone
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after abnormal flame condition is removed.

root_causes: Tubing leak or water in tubing
diagnostic_path: Verify pressure switch closes within allowed proving period
Per ML180UH public sequence, 2.5 minutes to prove then pressure-switch watchguard starts with 5-minute reset period
Measure operating pressure in inches w.c. at the switch
codes.escalation: Heat sequence aborted; watchguard engages if proving repeatedly missed
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-retry after reset period; manual thermostat interruption also resets sequence path in some branches.
codes.source: Lennox G61MPV diagnostic table, p.63; Lennox ML180UH heating sequence, p.40

root_causes: False proving due to tubing or water issue
diagnostic_path: Verify switch is open before inducer start
Confirm switch state against actual inducer operation
codes.escalation: Heat sequence will not proceed correctly
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once switch state is corrected and sequence is restarted.

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch opened during ignition trial or heating demand
diagnostic_path: Verify switch remains closed through ignition and run
Check vent, intake, and condensate path
Measure running pressure in inches w.c.
codes.escalation: Recycle / retry behavior; may lead to watchguard
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when pressure switch remains proven during a successful heat call.

diagnostic_path: Verify temperature rise and input rate
codes.escalation: Burner off; blower operation continues per sequence
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears after switch recloses.

codes.meaning: Watchguard — exceeded maximum number of retries
diagnostic_path: Per ML180UH public sequence, five consecutive failed flame-proving tries during one heat demand lead to watchguard and 60-minute reset period
Verify igniter, gas delivery, and flame current
codes.escalation: Watchguard; heating inhibited until reset period completes
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears after 60 minutes or after a successful subsequent heat call, depending on fault branch.
codes.source: Lennox G61MPV diagnostic table, p.63; Lennox ML180UH heating sequence, p.41

codes.meaning: Ignitor circuit fault — failed ignitor or triggering circuitry
diagnostic_path: Verify control output to ignitor circuit
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after successful ignition sequence or manual interruption of demand.

codes.meaning: Board fault detected (internal control self-check failure)
diagnostic_path: Reset control by turning off main power
If state returns after power cycle, replace control
codes.reset_behavior: Main power reset required.

codes.code_identifier: LED #1 fast flash / LED #2 slow flash
root_causes: Line hot-neutral reversed at incoming power
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after wiring corrected.
codes.source: Lennox ML180UH heating sequence, p.40; G61MPV code table p.63

platforms.platform_description: Current-generation Lennox residential gas furnace platform using 7-segment alphanumeric display. Covers EL296V (two-stage 96% AFUE), EL296UHV, SL280V/VN (two-stage 80% AFUE), SL297V (two-stage 97% AFUE), SLP99V (modulating 99% AFUE). Full namespace redesign from SureLight compound codes — NOT a carry-forward. Uses status characters ('.', 'A', 'C', 'H', 'h', 'dF', 'U') and E-codes spanning E105-E370+ with dedicated soft-lockout derivatives E270-E276 and reduced-capacity adaptive response E311. Communicating-capable via RSBus. Pushbutton menu exposes 'E' (recall last 10 errors), 'c' (clear error memory), 'F' (live flame-signal microamps).
platforms.visual_identification: Single 7-segment alphanumeric display on the integrated furnace control. Pushbutton access to diagnostic menu ('E' for error recall, 'c' for clear, 'F' for flame microamp live read). Display shows status characters during normal operation: '.' = idle (1 Hz blink), 'A' = active cooling, 'C' = cooling CFM, 'H' = heat stage with CFM, 'h' = first stage heat, 'dF' = defrost, 'U' = unavailable/configuration needed. Fault codes display as 'E' followed by 3-digit code (E105, E223, E270).
platforms.platform_notes: Primary public source: Lennox unit information doc 100027 (diagnostic code table, approximately pp.10-15). Namespace is dense with soft-lockout 'last cycle reason' codes (E270-E276) that explain WHY watchguard engaged. E311 is notable for adaptive capacity reduction in 60-second steps instead of hard lockout on airflow restriction. E347-E349 are specific to communicating furnace with non-communicating outdoor unit configurations.

diagnostic_path: Verify no W/Y/G demand if idle is unexpected
codes.reset_behavior: Not a fault.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, integrated diagnostic/status table, p.10

codes.meaning: Gas heat stage display; stage number (1 or 2) shown with CFM data
diagnostic_path: Verify stage displayed matches actual demand and airflow
codes.reset_behavior: Not a fault.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, status table, p.10

codes.meaning: Device communication problem — no other devices on RSBus
diagnostic_path: Check for nearby high-voltage noise source
codes.escalation: Communication fault; clears after bus communications are restored
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears after communications recover.

codes.escalation: Normal operation resumes once voltage returns
codes.reset_behavior: Clears 5 seconds after recovery.

codes.escalation: Control resumes normal operation after correction
codes.reset_behavior: Clears 5 seconds after recovery.
Clears 5 seconds after fault recovers.

diagnostic_path: Correct supply so line voltage returns to proper range
codes.escalation: Normal operation interrupted until voltage back in range
codes.reset_behavior: System resumes normal operation 5 seconds after fault recovered.

codes.meaning: Line voltage frequency out of range
root_causes: Power quality or source frequency problem
codes.escalation: Heating/cooling operation interrupted until acceptable power returns
codes.reset_behavior: System resumes normal operation 5 seconds after fault recovered.

codes.meaning: Low 24V — control will restart if error recovers
root_causes: 24V outside Lennox's stated 18 to 30 VAC range
Too much power-robbing load on system
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fault recovers.

root_causes: 24V control power above Lennox stated 18 to 30 VAC range
Improper line voltage feeding equipment transformer
diagnostic_path: Check line voltage feeding the equipment transformer
Correct voltage so control senses proper range
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when the control senses proper voltage.
Clears after device responds to inquiry.

codes.meaning: Active communicating thermostat signal missing for more than 3 minutes
codes.escalation: Stops services and waits for thermostat heartbeat
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when valid thermostat message is received.

codes.meaning: Control failed self-check / internal error / failed hardware
codes.reset_behavior: Clears 300 seconds after fault recovered.

diagnostic_path: If needed, execute factory default mode
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after proper configuration is restored.

root_causes: Sensor shorted or out of range
diagnostic_path: Compare sensor resistance to Lennox temperature/resistance chart
Reconfigure if feature should be marked not installed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears 30 seconds after recovery.

codes.meaning: Hard lockout — rollout circuit open or previously open
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fault recovered.
Clears after fault recovered.

diagnostic_path: Reset and reprogram with thermostat removed while applying power
codes.escalation: Fault until correct match is detected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after correct match is detected following reset.

diagnostic_path: Program valid unit size code per configuration guide
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after valid code is read following reset.

diagnostic_path: Check gas valve operation and wiring
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when repaired.

codes.meaning: Gas valve control relay contact shorted
diagnostic_path: Replace control if wiring is correct
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after repair or control replacement.

diagnostic_path: Confirm furnace only runs in first stage on call
Replace control if stage 2 cannot be restored
codes.escalation: Furnace operates on first stage for remainder of demand
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fault recovers.

diagnostic_path: Replace if open or outside Lennox IOM range
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after fault is cleared.

diagnostic_path: Check pressure switch closing pressure on heat call
Measure operating pressure in inches w.c.
Inspect vent and inducer for restriction or proper operation
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after fault clears.

diagnostic_path: Check if low-pressure switch stays closed beyond 150 seconds on heat call
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after fault clears.
Resumes normal operation after fault clears.

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch open during trial for ignition or run mode
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after fault clears.

root_causes: Pressure-switch calibration could not be completed
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after calibration/fault clears.

root_causes: IFC switched to high-fire ignition because low-fire pressure switch did not close in time
diagnostic_path: Verify low-fire pressure-switch proving and inducer operation
codes.escalation: Informational / compensating action; Lennox says no action needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears with successful operation.

diagnostic_path: Use control diagnostics or field-installed mode to read flame current in microamps
Measure neutral-to-ground voltage to verify grounding
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after current heat call completes.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, p.13; EL195UHNEK IOM p.47

codes.meaning: Flame sensed out of sequence — flame still present
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when fault is recovered.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, p.13; EL195UHNEK IOM p.47

codes.escalation: If limit does not close within 3 minutes, unit goes into 1-hour soft lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Resumes normal operation after fault clears.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, p.13; EL195UHNEK IOM p.47

codes.meaning: Discharge air temperature too high (gas heat only)
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call is finished.

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — exceeded maximum number of retries; no flame current sensed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, p.13; EL195UHNEK IOM p.47

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — exceeded retries; last retry failed due to pressure switch opening
diagnostic_path: Check pressure-switch closing pressure and running pressure
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — exceeded maximum number of recycles; last recycle due to pressure switch opening
diagnostic_path: Check low pressure switch operation to see if it is stuck closed on heat call
Check high pressure switch closing pressure on heat call
Measure operating pressure in inches w.c.
Inspect vent and combustion air inducer for correct operation and restriction
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when the heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — exceeded recycles; last recycle due to flame failure
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — exceeded recycles; last recycle failed due to limit opening or limit open >3 minutes
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Soft lockout — flame sensed out of sequence; flame signal is gone
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when flame has been proven stable.
Clears when calibration finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Ignitor circuit fault — failed ignitor or triggering circuitry
diagnostic_path: Replace if open or out of spec
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when flame has been proven stable.
codes.source: Lennox unit information doc 100027, p.14; EL195UHNEK IOM p.47

codes.meaning: Heat airflow restricted below the minimum
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Indoor blower motor unable to start due to obstructed wheel / seized bearings
diagnostic_path: Verify motor starts and meets performance
Replace motor or wheel if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after circulator successfully starts.

diagnostic_path: Check combustion blower bearings, wiring, and amperage
Replace if performance is out of range
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after inducer current returns in-range following ignition after soft lockout or reset.

codes.meaning: Indoor blower motor temperature too high
diagnostic_path: Check motor bearings and amp draw
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after blower demand is satisfied.

root_causes: Sensor shorted or out of range
diagnostic_path: Compare sensor resistance to installation-chart values
codes.reset_behavior: Communicating mode: clears 30 seconds after recovery; non-communicating mode: clears after current heat call completes.

codes.meaning: Heat rate reduced to match indoor blower airflow
diagnostic_path: Reduce restriction by checking filter and duct system
codes.escalation: Warning only; Lennox states two-stage controls reduce to first stage, and firing rate is reduced every 60 seconds to match available CFM
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when heat call finishes successfully.

codes.meaning: Restricted airflow in cooling or continuous fan mode is lower than CFM setting
root_causes: Total external static outside design range
diagnostic_path: Verify blower is not operating outside design parameters
codes.escalation: Warning only; indoor blower running at reduced CFM in cutback mode
codes.reset_behavior: Cleared after current service demand is satisfied.

codes.meaning: Indoor or outdoor unit capacity mismatch; communication only
diagnostic_path: Check equipment configuration in installation instructions
Correct capacity coding during setup / commissioning
codes.escalation: Warning only; system can still operate but may not meet intended efficiency or capacity
codes.reset_behavior: Alarm clears when commissioning is exited / completed.

codes.meaning: No 24V output on Y1 of integrated control with non-communicating outdoor unit
root_causes: Y1 relay / stage-1 pilot relay contacts did not close
No input back to IFC chip
diagnostic_path: Check Y1 output from integrated control
If relay logic/output has failed, replace integrated control
codes.reset_behavior: Cleared after reset and Y1 input is sensed.

codes.meaning: No 24V output on Y2 of integrated control with non-communicating outdoor unit
root_causes: Y2 relay / stage-2 pilot relay contacts did not close
No input back to IFC chip
diagnostic_path: Check Y2 output from integrated control
Replace integrated control if relay/output fault is confirmed
codes.reset_behavior: Cleared after reset and Y1 input is sensed.

codes.meaning: No 24V between R and O on integrated control with non-communicating outdoor unit; dual-fuel module required for heat-pump application
diagnostic_path: Verify heat-pump / dual-fuel wiring and configuration
Restore or hardwire the R-to-O link as required
codes.escalation: Critical alert in applicable non-communicating configuration
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once the required configuration link is restored.

codes.meaning: Interlock switch sensed open for 2 minutes
root_causes: Loss of 24VAC seen by the control on the DS/interlock path
Open interlock / door / safety switch circuit
diagnostic_path: Verify continuity of the interlock/DS circuit
Restore continuous 24VAC to the DS terminal
codes.escalation: Control terminates all services and waits for interlock switch to close
codes.reset_behavior: Alarm clears when 24VAC is continuously sensed on DS for at least 10 seconds or on power reset.

platforms.platform_description: K-revision furnaces compliant with A2L refrigerant transition (R-454B). Verified public models: EL195UHNEK, ML196UHEK, SLP99UHVK. Retain 7-segment IFC and inherit most Platform 2 E-codes (E105-E370 legacy furnace codes carry forward), BUT add a genuinely new A2L refrigerant-leak-detection code family: E150, E151, E152, E154, E155, E160, E161, E163, E164, E390.
platforms.visual_identification: Same 7-segment display as Platform 2, but furnace has additional hardware: refrigerant leak detection (RDS) sensors typically mounted at or near the indoor coil, plus a Low GWP test pushbutton on the control board. Dedicated low-GWP dip switches on control.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Lennox EL195UHNEK Installation Instructions 508575-01, pp.46-47. Legacy furnace codes (E105-E370 from Platform 2) still apply to this platform — only the E150-E164 and E390 codes are genuinely new. RDS-specific codes have mandated behaviors (E154/E155 latch blower for minimum 5 minutes regardless of thermostat call). A2L refrigerant detection CANNOT be disabled per Lennox safety code requirement.

diagnostic_path: Repair leak at or near indoor unit coil as needed
Do not expect code to clear while sensor still reports leak
codes.reset_behavior: Cannot be cleared while sensor still reports a leak.

root_causes: Sensor failed or reporting internal fault
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when sensor no longer reports fault condition.

root_causes: Sensor #2 failed or reporting a sensor fault
diagnostic_path: Replace sensor if the fault persists
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when sensor no longer reports the fault condition.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detector sensor #1 communication lost or invalid dip-switch configuration
diagnostic_path: Check sensor harness and connector condition
Check plug for damage or debris
Retest using Low GWP test button on control board
codes.escalation: Blower latches for a minimum of 5 minutes
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when sensor communication is reestablished; blower remains latched for at least 5 minutes regardless.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detector sensor #2 communication lost
root_causes: Harness or connector issue on sensor #2
diagnostic_path: Retest with Low GWP test button
codes.escalation: Blower latches for a minimum of 5 minutes
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when communication is reestablished; blower remains latched for at least 5 minutes regardless.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detector sensor #1 type incorrect
diagnostic_path: Retest with Low GWP test button
codes.escalation: Blower latches for a minimum of 5 minutes
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when a suitable sensor is detected.

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detector sensor #2 type incorrect
root_causes: Wrong sensor type installed on low-GWP input #2
diagnostic_path: Replace with the correct Lennox-approved sensor type
Retest using the Low GWP test button if needed
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when a suitable sensor is detected.

root_causes: Refrigerant detection control failure / board-level low-GWP control problem
diagnostic_path: Replace the failed low-GWP control / board if the fault persists
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when the control fault is corrected.

root_causes: Manual activation of Low GWP test button
diagnostic_path: Confirm code was intentionally invoked as a test function
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears after 1 minute.

diagnostic_path: Replace indoor unit control board if relay fault persists
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when relay operates normally.

platforms.platform_description: Lennox communicating ecosystem built around S30, S40, E30 color touchscreen thermostats plus communicating indoor and outdoor equipment. Uses three-surface diagnostic architecture: (1) wall thermostat shows alert codes 10-39 for commissioning/config and 400+ for equipment-sourced faults; (2) outdoor inverter units (XC20, XC25, XP20, XP25, XP18, XC18) generate inverter flash codes that bridge to thermostat alert numbers — a single event carries THREE linked IDs: thermostat number + inverter flash count + red/green LED flash pattern; (3) communicating indoor equipment (CBX32MV air handler, communicating furnaces) inherits Platform 2 E-codes.
platforms.visual_identification: Color touchscreen wall thermostat (S30/S40/E30) displays system alerts. Dealer Control Center (S30) or View Service Support Center (S40) menus expose diagnostics. Outdoor communicating inverter units have local LEDs that flash inverter-specific codes with distinct red/green flash patterns. Equipment reset paths differ by thermostat generation.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c; iComfort S30 Installation Manual and Setup Guide. Public alert guide is curated — documents most common commissioning/field alerts (10-39) and meaningful slice of outdoor inverter alerts (429-437 range plus algorithmic codes). Comprehensive per-model AOD dictionary not publicly available. A single outdoor inverter event carries THREE identifiers — thermostat number, inverter flash count, AND red/green LED flash pattern — preserve all three.

root_causes: New communicating device added after original setup
diagnostic_path: Confirm wiring between thermostat, smart hub, and devices
S30 path: Menu > Settings > Advanced Settings > View Dealer Control Center > Equipment > Reset / Re-configure System
codes.escalation: Thermostat may soft-disable the unknown device; furnace/air-handler/outdoor controls may show double horizontal lines; damper/EIM green LED may flash 3 seconds on / 1 second off
codes.reset_behavior: Clear by reconfiguring the system.
codes.source: iComfort S30 Installation Manual and Setup Guide, pp.53-54; Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8

root_causes: Previously installed communicating component not found
diagnostic_path: Check all device connections and Lennox compatibility
codes.reset_behavior: Cycle power; if persistent, clear by reconfiguring system.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, pp.7-8; S30 manual p.54

diagnostic_path: Check for voltage and missing component
Check R, i+, i-, C at mag-mount/subbase, smart hub, and all attached devices
Cycle power to indoor unit first, then thermostat
Verify EIM configured as furnace or air handler if used with non-communicating indoor unit
Replace indoor unit control if no response
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8

root_causes: Duplicate wireless/comfort-sensor identity or duplicate device presence
diagnostic_path: Check wiring and remove duplicate equipment
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8; S30 manual p.55

codes.meaning: Too Many Devices of the Same Type
root_causes: More than one thermostat, indoor unit, or outdoor unit present
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8; S30 manual p.55

diagnostic_path: Re-set system and start configuration again
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once proper configuration is completed.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8

root_causes: Thermostat firmware too old for one of the system devices
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after thermostat firmware is updated.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8

root_causes: Mismatch like single-stage non-variable-speed furnace with modulating outdoor unit
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when compatible system configuration is restored.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.8

codes.meaning: Must Program Unit Capacity For Device
root_causes: Indoor or outdoor device missing tonnage programming
diagnostic_path: Remove power to thermostat before programming the unit control
Program capacity manually at the applicable unit control
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.9

root_causes: Device operating in a way that does not match expected command/state
diagnostic_path: S30: Dealer Control Center > Diagnostics > Start
S40: View Service Support Center > Diagnostics > Select All > Start
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.10

root_causes: Cooling has run for at least 15 minutes without demand
diagnostic_path: Determine cause of unintended cooling demand
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.10

root_causes: System unable to start heating for more than 45 minutes
codes.escalation: System goes offline for 60 minutes and attempts restart
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.11

root_causes: System unable to start cooling for more than 45 minutes
codes.escalation: System goes offline for 60 minutes and attempts restart
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.11

codes.code_identifier: Inverter Normal State — Red LED ON / Green LED OFF
codes.meaning: Normal inverter operation with no error code present
diagnostic_path: If the outdoor unit is not operating normally despite this LED state, move to thermostat alert history and dealer diagnostics rather than treating the inverter LEDs themselves as faulted
codes.reset_behavior: Not a fault.
codes.source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c, pp.35-41

codes.meaning: OD Inverter DC Link Low Voltage
codes.escalation: Service soon or service urgent depending on event history
codes.reset_behavior: Auto clears when issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, pp.34-35

diagnostic_path: Replace outdoor control board if indicated
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit stops compressor and fan; anti-short-cycle starts; if this happens 10 times in 60 consecutive minutes, system locks out to service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: Disconnect power to outdoor unit and restart.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.35

root_causes: PFC circuit over-current; guide states 100A peak event threshold
diagnostic_path: Refer to detailed outdoor unit service procedure
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit stops; anti-short-cycle starts; if repeated 10 times in 60 consecutive minutes, system locks out to service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: Disconnect power to outdoor unit and restart.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.35

codes.meaning: OD Inverter DC Link High Voltage
root_causes: DC link capacitor voltage too high; guide states greater than 480 VDC
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit stops; anti-short-cycle starts; if repeated 10 times in 60 consecutive minutes, system locks out to service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: Disconnect power to outdoor unit and restart.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.36

codes.code_identifier: 433 / Inverter Flash 29 — Red LED 2 flashes / Green LED 9 flashes
diagnostic_path: Refer to unit service documentation for detailed troubleshooting
Check compressor and inverter condition per outdoor-unit service procedure
codes.escalation: Outdoor control treats repeated occurrences as service-soon/service-urgent behavior in the communicating alert framework
codes.reset_behavior: To clear alert code, disconnect power to both the indoor and outdoor units, then reconnect power and restart.
codes.source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c, p.38

codes.code_identifier: 434 / Inverter Flash 53 — Red LED 5 flashes / Green LED 3 flashes
root_causes: Interruption of main power to inverter
diagnostic_path: Check for loose or disconnected electrical connections
Verify stable power to the inverter and outdoor section
Use detailed outdoor-unit service documentation if the fault persists
codes.escalation: If repeated to lockout threshold, outdoor unit locks out and displays alert code 434 as service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: Service-soon condition automatically clears when the system detects the issue no longer exists; if locked out with service-urgent 434, reset by cycling outdoor-unit power off/on first, then cycling indoor 24VAC power to the Lennox outdoor control off/on.
codes.source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c, p.39

codes.code_identifier: 435 / Inverter Flash 60 — Red LED 6 flashes / Green LED OFF
diagnostic_path: Follow outdoor-unit service documentation for inverter internal fault diagnosis
codes.escalation: Outdoor control cycles power to the inverter by opening the contactor for 2 minutes as service-soon behavior; it will cycle power three times and then lock out the outdoor unit as service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: To clear alert code, disconnect power to the outdoor unit and restart.
codes.source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c, p.40

codes.code_identifier: 436 / Inverter Flash 62 — Red LED 6 flashes / Green LED 2 flashes
root_causes: Inverter heat sink temperature exceeded limit
Loose screws reducing thermal contact between heat sink and inverter control board
diagnostic_path: Check and tighten screws that hold the heat sink to the inverter control board
Wait 5 minutes for all capacitors to discharge before checking screws
Use outdoor-unit service documentation for further diagnosis
codes.escalation: Inverter first issues alert code 13 and slows down to cool the heat sink; if temperature remains high, outdoor unit stops compressor and fan as service-soon, anti-short-cycle starts, and if the condition occurs 5 times within 1 hour the system locks out as service urgent
codes.reset_behavior: Service-soon condition automatically clears when the inverter sends an alert-code-clear message; service-urgent condition is cleared by disconnecting power to the outdoor unit and restarting.
codes.source: Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c, p.40

codes.meaning: OD Inverter Heat-Sink Temperature Sensor Fault
root_causes: Heat-sink sensor reading below 4°F or above 264°F after 10 minutes of operation
diagnostic_path: Check sensor condition and outdoor inverter section
codes.escalation: Outdoor unit stops compressor and fan
codes.reset_behavior: Service-soon condition auto clears when inverter sends clear message; service-urgent condition clears by disconnecting and reconnecting outdoor power.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.40

root_causes: Outdoor fan service issue; guide ties event to superheat/suction values not in expected range
codes.reset_behavior: Automatically clears when system detects issue no longer exists.
codes.source: Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide, p.49

subcode_retrieval_procedure.tool_required: iComfort Mobile Setup application for service workflow
navigation_steps: On thermostat: Menu > Settings > Advanced Settings > Dealer Control Center > Notifications
Review active alarms on homeowner notification screen or in Dealer Control Center > Notifications
For service-app access, press the smart hub commissioning button once
Connect the mobile device to the smart hub when the LED begins blinking green
Use the app as a service tool after system commissioning is already complete (S30 service session auto-logs out after 30 minutes)

fault_history_buffer.clear_procedure: Alert codes requiring manual clearing must be cleared under Dealer Control Center > Notifications

cross_generation_conflicts.description: SureLight uses COMPOUND X+Y flash codes, NOT single-digit counts

meanings.meaning: Compound X+Y: pattern is 'X flashes, pause, Y flashes' (e.g., '1+2' = 1 flash, pause, 2 flashes = low flame run). Techs who count total red flashes as a single number read the codes wrong.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Lennox SureLight boards use COMPOUND flash codes, not single counts. If you see a red LED flashing 2-pause-3, that's code '2+3' (low pressure switch failed open) — NOT code 5 or code 23. Watch for the pause between flash groups.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Low flame current (run mode) has different identifier across generations

meanings.meaning: Compound code '1+2' = low flame current during run mode
E240 = low flame current during run mode (same concept, different identifier)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Low flame current shows as '1+2' on SureLight boards (G61MPV, ML180UH) but as 'E240' on newer 7-segment boards (EL296V, SLP99V). Same diagnostic path — clean sensor, verify grounding, check flame microamps — but the codes look completely different.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Pressure switch faults split into more granular codes on 7-segment vs SureLight

meanings.meaning: SureLight: '2+3' failed open, '2+4' failed closed, '2+7' opened during run
7-segment: E223 failed open, E224 failed closed (150-second detection window explicit), E227 open during trial/run — plus dedicated soft-lockout derivatives E271 and E272

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Pressure switch faults map across generations but the 7-segment platform adds explicit timing (150 seconds for failed-closed recognition) and separate soft-lockout codes E271/E272 that specifically name 'pressure switch' as the reason.

meanings.meaning: SureLight calls it 'Watchguard' — compound code 4+1 = exceeded max retries, 60-minute auto-reset

7-segment calls it 'Soft Lockout' and splits by cause: E270 (no flame), E271 (pressure switch retry), E272 (pressure switch recycle), E273 (flame failure), E274 (limit), E275 (flame out of sequence), E276 (calibration)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: SureLight watchguard (code '4+1', 60-minute reset) maps to 7-segment soft lockouts, but the newer platform names WHY: E270 through E276 each explain the specific failure mode.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Low-GWP K-revision adds entirely new A2L refrigerant detection code family not present in pre-K 7-segment

meanings.meaning: E150-E164 and E390 do NOT exist on pre-K (EL296V, SL280V, SL297V, SLP99V) platforms — throwing any of these codes indicates a K-rev board

E150 = leak detected (cannot clear while leak present); E151/E152 = sensor #1/#2 fault; E154/E155 = sensor #1/#2 comm lost (blower latches minimum 5 minutes); E160/E161 = sensor #1/#2 wrong type; E163 = leak detector control failure; E164 = test mode; E390 = Low GWP relay stuck

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Codes E150, E151, E152, E154, E155, E160, E161, E163, E164, and E390 only exist on K-revision Lennox furnaces (EL195UHNEK, ML196UHEK). These are refrigerant leak detection codes for R-454B A2L compliance. E154/E155 will latch the blower ON for at least 5 minutes regardless of thermostat call — this is a code-mandated safety behavior. A2L refrigerant detection CANNOT be disabled — it's prohibited by safety codes.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: iComfort outdoor inverter events carry THREE linked identifiers on different surfaces
cross_generation_conflicts.code_identifier: Alert number / Inverter flash / LED flash pattern

meanings.meaning: Same event on different surfaces with THREE identifiers: thermostat shows number (e.g., 434), outdoor inverter flashes count (e.g., Flash 53), and red/green LED pattern matches (5 red flashes / 3 green flashes for 434). Examples: 433 / Flash 29 / Red 2 Green 9 (compressor over-current); 435 / Flash 60 / Red 6 Green OFF (EEPROM checksum). Reset procedures can differ between service-soon and service-urgent — 433 requires disconnecting both indoor and outdoor units; 434 service-urgent requires two-step reset (outdoor first, then indoor 24VAC).

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: iComfort outdoor inverter faults appear on THREE linked surfaces with DIFFERENT identifiers. Thermostat shows a number (434); outdoor unit's inverter LEDs flash a count (Flash 53); red/green flash patterns match the inverter flash number (5 red / 3 green). Same event — three IDs. Always check both thermostat alert history AND outdoor inverter LED pattern.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: iComfort alerts 38/39 trigger 60-minute offline retry, not lockout

meanings.meaning: Alert 38 (not heating after 45 minutes) and Alert 39 (not cooling after 45 minutes): system marks itself offline for 60 minutes and auto-restarts. NOT a hard lockout.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: iComfort alerts 38 and 39 trigger a 60-minute 'system offline' period with automatic restart — NOT a lockout requiring manual reset. Customer calling about 'thermostat went blank / system is dead' may actually be in the 60-minute offline retry window and will self-restart.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: Lennox CBX32MV (communicating air handler) is the primary electric-heat platform in the iComfort ecosystem. It inherits most 7-segment E-code architecture plus communicating-specific codes that flow through to the thermostat. Non-communicating Lennox/Allied electric furnaces use conventional sequencer logic without formal fault code dictionaries — service is component-level (open element, failed sequencer, limit trip). Platform 2's E250 (limit circuit open) and E291 (heat airflow restricted below minimum) are the closest equivalents for airflow-related electric-heat faults on communicating platforms.
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Open element on multi-stage EH packages
Failed sequencer on non-communicating air handlers
Airflow-related limit trip — on 7-segment IFC this throws E250/E274
24V transformer or fuse failure (iComfort treats as comm loss E201)
Low-voltage short causing repeated fuse failure
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Not applicable — Lennox/Allied does not have a significant manufactured-home-specific product line.

metadata.source_note: Compiled from public Lennox documentation: G61MPV Installation Instructions p.63 (SureLight diagnostic table); Lennox ML180UH heating sequence pp.40-41 (watchguard timing); Lennox unit information doc 100027 pp.10-15 (7-segment diagnostic code table); Lennox EL195UHNEK Installation Instructions 508575-01 pp.46-47 (Low-GWP RDS code table); iComfort S30 Installation Manual pp.53-55; Lennox Residential Communicating Systems Alert Code Guide 100017c pp.7-49. Three research passes with final accuracy correction. Allied Air brands likely alias Platforms 1-2 on shared hardware but per-brand dictionaries are thinner in public sources.
